title: forge_approve_perspective must require human-in-the-loop review
description:
forge_approve_perspective currently allows an AI agent to approve perspectives without human verification. This is dangerous — perspectives are derived by AI from bullets, and approving them without human review means hallucinated or inaccurate content can flow directly into resumes.

## Problem
An MCP client (Claude) can call forge_prepare_derivation → forge_commit_derivation → forge_approve_perspective in a single automated flow. The approve step has no gate requiring the human to actually read and verify the perspective content before it becomes resume-ready.

## Risk
- AI-generated perspectives may contain hallucinated accomplishments
- Subtle inaccuracies (wrong metrics, inflated scope) pass unchecked
- Approved perspectives are added to resumes and exported to PDF/LaTeX
- User's professional reputation depends on accuracy of these statements

## Proposed fix
forge_approve_perspective should:
1. Return a confirmation prompt that includes the full perspective content
2. Require the human to explicitly confirm after seeing the content
3. Consider adding a "reviewed_by" field (human vs agent) to track provenance
4. Optionally: require perspectives to sit in "pending_review" for a minimum time before approval is allowed

## Workaround
Current mitigation: use forge_review_pending to list all pending perspectives, manually review each, then approve individually. But nothing enforces this workflow.

title: Retrofit tests: acceptance, component, and contract coverage for already-landed phases
description:
Option C retrofit from the Qualifications planning session (2026-04-05). The
Qualifications track going forward is following Option A (explicit
acceptance tests + component tests + contract tests per task). This issue
captures the equivalent retrofit work for phases that have already landed:

Missing acceptance / component / contract tests on main:

1. **Phase 89 (Skills Expansion)** — merged 2026-04-05

   - Existing coverage is implementation tests (skill repo, junction CRUD,
     migration-031). Gap: assert each "Acceptance Criteria" bullet from
     the plan maps to a test case.

2. **Phase 91 (Summaries Rework)** — merged 2026-04-05
   - No structural component test for `SummaryDetailModal.svelte` (modal
     state, skill picker, industry/role_type dropdowns).
   - No structural component test for the rewritten
     `/resumes/summaries/+page.svelte` (sticky headers, group-by,
     sort-by, clickable rows).
   - No acceptance tests for T91.1-T91.6 criteria.

3. **Phase 92 (Tagline Engine)** — merged 2026-04-05
   - No structural component test for `HeaderEditor.svelte` rewrite
     (override vs generated display, regenerate button, reset).
   - Tagline service has strong unit tests (27) — fine.
   - Integration tests for JD link/unlink regeneration exist — fine.
   - Gap: contract tests for the new `/resumes/:id/tagline` endpoints.

4. **Phase 94 (Resume Source Tab Rendering)** — merged earlier
   - No component tests for the rendered markdown / LaTeX preview paths.

Pattern to follow: the project's convention is **structural** component
tests (read source file + assert on patterns with regex/string-match),
not runtime rendering. See:
- packages/webui/src/__tests__/jd-overlay.test.ts
- packages/webui/src/__tests__/component-adoption.test.ts
- packages/webui/src/__tests__/interactive-systems.test.ts

For contract tests, follow the existing pattern in
packages/core/src/routes/__tests__/contracts.test.ts.

Blocks: nothing urgent — this is maintenance. Pick up after the
Qualifications track ships and before the next feature rollout.

title: Design: Rust implementation phases, seams, and migration strategy

Create child beads for each implementation batch.
design:
Key questions to resolve:
1. Implementation order: which domain first? IR compiler is pure computation. Source/Bullet are simple CRUD. Resume is most complex.
2. Testing strategy: unit tests per service, integration tests against real SQLite. Test fixtures reusable from TS?
3. DB migration: share schema with TS (same migrate.ts output) or Rust-native migrations?
4. SDK API surface: does forge-sdk export a single Forge struct (facade) or individual services?
5. Error types: ForgeError enum shared across crates or per-crate errors?
6. Seam between sdk and server: axum extractors wrapping sdk types directly?

title: Research: Rust crate selection for forge-sdk and forge-ai
description: Evaluate and select Rust crates for key dependencies. Domains: SQLite access (sqlx vs rusqlite vs diesel), LaTeX/PDF compilation (tectonic vs pdflatex subprocess vs typst), LLM client (anthropic-rs vs raw reqwest), async runtime (tokio vs none for library code), serialization patterns (serde best practices for SQLite row mapping). Output: ADR per decision with rationale, benchmarks where relevant.
design:
Research areas:
1. SQLite: sqlx (async, compile-time checked) vs rusqlite (sync, mature, lighter). Consider: forge is single-user, async may be unnecessary overhead.
2. LaTeX: tectonic (pure Rust, no system deps) vs pdflatex subprocess (requires TeX install). Tectonic is ~200MB but self-contained.
3. LLM: anthropic-rs crate (if exists and maintained) vs raw reqwest + serde. Check crate maturity.
4. Async: tokio everywhere vs sync core + async only at server boundary. Library code (forge-sdk) may not need async.
5. Error handling: thiserror vs anyhow vs custom enum. Probably thiserror for library, anyhow for binaries.

title: Stub: forge-sdk — IR Compiler
description: Stub the resume IR compiler pipeline. The compiler transforms resume data (sections, entries, perspectives, skills) into an intermediate representation, then renders to LaTeX/PDF. Most complex service in the codebase.
design:
TS Source:
- packages/core/src/services/resume-compiler-service.ts
- packages/core/src/ir/ (if exists)

Key behaviors:
- Fetch resume with all nested data (header, sections, entries, perspectives)
- Build IR: normalized tree structure independent of output format
- Render IR to LaTeX (template-based)
- LaTeX to PDF (subprocess: pdflatex/tectonic)
- Profile data merged into header
- Skill categories grouped and ordered per resume settings
- Section ordering preserved
- Handles missing data gracefully (empty sections omitted)

Rust divergences:
- IR as a Rust enum tree (not JSON objects)
- LaTeX escaping as a pure function
- PDF subprocess via std::process::Command
- This is the best candidate for first real implementation (R0) — pure computation, easily testable

- [ ] LaTeX rendering pipeline documented

title: Resume lifecycle statuses: backlog → draft → review → approved/rejected → submitted → archived
description:
Resumes currently have no status field — they exist as static documents with no lifecycle tracking. Add a status field that models the resume pipeline from creation through submission.

## Proposed statuses
- **backlog** — created but not actively being worked on
- **draft** — actively being built/edited
- **review** — content complete, awaiting human review
- **approved** — reviewed and ready to submit
- **rejected** — reviewed and needs rework (returns to draft)
- **submitted** — sent to employer/application
- **archived** — no longer active (position filled, withdrawn, etc.)

## Transitions
```
backlog → draft → review → approved → submitted → archived
                    ↓
                 rejected → draft (rework cycle)
```

## Impact
- Migration: add `status TEXT DEFAULT 'draft'` to resumes table + CHECK constraint
- Service: add status transition validation (e.g., can't go from backlog to submitted)
- MCP: add forge_update_resume_status tool
- UI: show status badge on resume cards, filter by status on /resumes
- Export: only allow PDF export from approved/submitted status (optional gate)
- Pairs with HITL perspective approval (forge-4g9l) — review status could gate on all perspectives being approved

title: Skill alternative spellings / match patterns
description:
Skills need alternative spellings or match patterns (regex/glob) for reliable extraction from JD text.

## Problem
JDs use inconsistent naming: "Kubernetes", "K8s", "k8s", "CI/CD", "CICD", "CI-CD", "Amazon Web Services", "AWS". The skill extractor needs to recognize all variants as the same skill.

## Proposed
Add a `skill_patterns` table:
```sql
CREATE TABLE skill_patterns (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  skill_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skills(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  pattern TEXT NOT NULL,
  pattern_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'literal' CHECK (pattern_type IN ('literal', 'regex', 'glob')),
  UNIQUE(skill_id, pattern)
);
```

Each skill can have multiple patterns. The extractor checks all patterns when scanning JD text. Literal patterns use case-insensitive exact match. Regex/glob for more complex cases (e.g., `CI[\/-]?CD`).

## Related
- Skill relations (aliases overlap with this — an alias is a named alternative, a pattern is a text-match rule)
- JD skill extraction pipeline

title: Skill extraction should use existing skills data for matching
description:
The JD skill extraction process should be aware of the existing skills table (and skill_patterns once added) when extracting skills from job description text.

## Current state
Skill extraction produces raw text strings that are then matched or created as skills. The extractor doesn't consult the existing skills inventory, leading to duplicates and missed matches.

## Proposed
When extracting skills from a JD:
1. Load existing skills + patterns into a lookup index
2. Match JD text against known skills first (exact, alias, pattern)
3. Only create new skills for genuinely novel terms
4. Flag uncertain matches for user review

This prevents the "Docker" vs "docker" vs "Docker Engine" proliferation problem.

title: Skill relations — alias, parent, child, sibling, alternative
description:
Add a relations system to skills so they can express semantic relationships to other skills.

## Relation types
- **alias**: same skill, different name (e.g., "K8s" ↔ "Kubernetes")
- **parent**: broader category (e.g., "Kubernetes" parent of "Helm")
- **child**: inverse of parent
- **sibling**: same level, related (e.g., "Docker" ↔ "Podman")
- **alternative**: interchangeable for job matching (e.g., "AWS" ↔ "GCP" ↔ "Azure")

## Proposed schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE skill_relations (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  skill_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skills(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  related_skill_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skills(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  relation_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (relation_type IN ('alias', 'parent', 'child', 'sibling', 'alternative')),
  UNIQUE(skill_id, related_skill_id, relation_type)
);
```

## Impact
- Gap analysis: alias/alternative matches should count as coverage
- Skills graph: richer edges
- Dedup: alias detection during skill creation

title: JD qualifications extraction — delineate certs/clearances from skills
description:
Add a qualifications dimension to job descriptions, parallel to the existing JD skills extraction. Currently all requirements are treated as skills, but certifications (Security+, CISSP) and clearances (TS/SCI) are qualifications — not skills.

## Problem
When a JD mentions "Security+" or "TS/SCI", these get extracted as skills and show up in the skills gap analysis. But they should be matched against the credentials/certifications tables, not the skills table. This conflates two different dimensions of job fit.

## Proposed
- Add a `job_description_qualifications` table (parallel to `job_description_skills`)
- Link JD qualifications to credentials/certifications for gap matching
- Update JD ingestion to classify requirements as skill vs qualification
- Update gap analysis to report qualification gaps separately from skill gaps

## Related
- Credentials + Certifications tables exist (migration 037)
- JD skills extraction: `job_description_skills` table
- Gap analysis: `forge_gap_analysis` MCP tool

title: UX for correcting a skill gap in a JD
description:
Add a user flow for resolving skill gaps identified in a JD. When the gap analysis shows a missing skill, the user needs a path to address it — either by linking an existing bullet/source that demonstrates the skill, or by acknowledging it as a true gap.

## Current state
Gap analysis shows which skills from a JD are not covered by the resume. But there is no UX to act on this — the user has to manually search for bullets, create perspectives, and add entries.

## Proposed UX
From the JD skill gap view, allow the user to:
1. Search existing bullets/perspectives that demonstrate the skill
2. Link a matching perspective to the resume (one-click add)
3. Mark a gap as "acknowledged" (true gap, no coverage exists)
4. Mark a gap as "not applicable" (skill listed in JD but not relevant)

title: JD skills UI: show Covered vs Gaps instead of full Required + Gaps
description:
Change the JD skills display in the web UI. Currently shows two lists:
- "Required Skills" (all skills from JD)
- "Skill Gaps" (subset not covered)

This is redundant — the full required list includes both covered and uncovered skills with no visual distinction.

## Proposed
Replace with a single view showing two groups:
- **Covered** — skills from the JD that are present in the linked resume (with checkmark or green indicator)
- **Gaps** — skills from the JD that are missing (with warning indicator)

This gives immediate visual feedback on coverage without needing to cross-reference two lists.

title: Extension: Inject 'Capture to Forge' sibling button via aria-label anchoring
description:
Inject a 'Capture to Forge' button next to LinkedIn's action buttons (Save, Share, More options) using aria-label selectors instead of fragile CSS class names or XPath.

Why aria-label over class selectors:
- Class names like '_8e681e09 _4e228146 f3c160da' are CSS module hashes that change on every LinkedIn CSS rebuild
- aria-label='Save' / aria-label='More options' is a semantic accessibility contract (WCAG) - changing it breaks screen readers and LinkedIn's own a11y test suite
- Orders of magnitude more stable: changes only on intentional redesign, not CSS rebuilds

Implementation:
- Find anchor: document.querySelector('button[aria-label="Save"]') or similar
- Inject sibling button styled to match LinkedIn's button aesthetic
- MutationObserver to handle SPA navigation (LinkedIn doesn't do full page reloads)
- Content script must run on LinkedIn job page load (not just on-demand)

Localization caveat: aria-label values are localized (French: 'Plus d options'). For now target English only. Future: label map per locale or fallback to aria-label existence + SVG path matching.

Fallback: If aria-label anchor not found (layout redesign), degrade gracefully to context menu only (the A dependency).
close_reason: Merged M2: injected button via aria-label

title: L1: Heading-based section splitter
description:
Split JD raw text into sections by detecting heading patterns: markdown ## headings, HTML h1-h6 tags, bold/strong text on its own line, ALL CAPS lines, and colon-terminated labels. Output: array of { heading: string | null, content: string, startIndex: number } sections. Should handle: - Markdown: '## Responsibilities', '### Required Skills' - HTML: '

About the Role

' - Bold: '**What You Will Do**' - ALL CAPS: 'RESPONSIBILITIES' - Colon labels: 'Responsibilities:' - No heading (preamble/inline content): heading = null

title: L2: Section taxonomy classifier (keyword heuristics)
description:
Classify each section from L1 output into the taxonomy using two strategies:

1. Heading match: fuzzy match heading text against known patterns per category
2. Keyword density: for sections with no heading or ambiguous headings, count domain keywords in the content body

Taxonomy and keyword signals:
- responsibilities: 'design', 'build', 'lead', 'own', 'develop', 'implement', 'work with'
- requirements: 'experience', 'years', 'degree', 'bachelor', 'proficiency', 'knowledge of'
- preferred: 'preferred', 'nice to have', 'bonus', 'plus', 'ideally'
- description: 'about the role', 'in this role', 'you will', 'the team'
- compensation: dollar amounts, 'salary', 'base', 'equity', 'bonus', 'range'
- location: city/state patterns, 'remote', 'hybrid', 'on-site', 'office'
- benefits: '401k', 'PTO', 'insurance', 'vacation', 'leave'
- about_company: 'about us', 'who we are', 'our mission', company name patterns
- eeo: 'equal opportunity', 'affirmative action', 'disability', 'accommodation'

Output: array of { heading, content, category, confidence } with confidence: 'high' (heading match) | 'medium' (keyword) | 'low' (fallback/unclassified)

title: Inline content extractors (salary, location, work posture)
description:
Extract structured data from section content, especially from sections where the data is inline (not under its own heading). Three extractors:

1. Salary extractor: regex for dollar ranges ('$147,000-$211,000'), 'base salary range', 'compensation'. Extract min, max, currency, type (base/total/hourly).

2. Location extractor: city/state patterns ('Reston, VA'), 'Remote', 'Hybrid (IN: 3, OFF: 2)'. Handle multiple locations ('Kirkland, WA; Sunnyvale, CA'). Extract as array of { city, state, country, posture }.

3. Work posture extractor: 'Remote', 'Hybrid', 'On-site', 'In-person'. May be inline in location or in a separate 'Work Type' section.

These run after L2 classification and can cross-reference multiple sections. Salary buried in a benefits paragraph should still be found.

title: Extension: JD Structural Parser
description:
Parse job descriptions into classified sections using a layered approach: (1) heading-based splitting, (2) keyword density classification, (3) optional small-model fallback. Enables salary extraction (3bp.15), location extraction (3bp.18), and future JD analysis.

Three-layer hybrid:
- L1: Structural parser (regex) - split by headings (markdown ##, bold, ALL CAPS, HTML h-tags)
- L2: Heuristic classifier (keyword density) - classify ambiguous sections by domain keywords
- L3: Small model fallback (optional) - Qwen2.5-0.5B or fine-tuned MiniLM for unclassified sections

Should live in shared code (not extension-only) - Forge core JD ingestion can reuse the same parser.

Section taxonomy: responsibilities, requirements, preferred, description, location, compensation, benefits, about_company, eeo

title: Extension: JD location → Org Campus linkage

When no org is set, location stays as a plain string.

title: LinkedIn plugin: location + salary chip selector drift across layouts
description: LinkedIn plugin extracts title/company/description correctly, but misses or misclassifies location and salary on the SDUI chip layout when chip order differs from the first fixture.
notes:
Reproducer: https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/4386859589/ (Capital One — Applied Researcher II)

Expected:
- location: 'McLean, VA' (or similar — real salary shown in description)
- salary_range: '$262,500 - $299,600' (from description body — this variant doesn't show salary as a chip at all)

Got:
- location: null
- salary_range: 'Full-time' (plugin picked up the employment-type chip, not a salary)

Root cause:
Current plugin uses svg[id='check-small'] sibling index 0 for salary and index 1 for location. On Crossing Hurdles fixture this worked because chip order was [salary, location]. On Capital One, chip order is [employment-type, workplace-type, experience-level, ...] with no salary chip at all. Location isn't a check-small chip on this page either.

Fix direction:
1. Capture Capital One page as tests/fixtures/linkedin/job-detail-capital-one.html
2. Add test case asserting expected location + salary (both can be null for this page — 'Full-time' must NOT appear as salary)
3. Rewrite chip classification: don't use positional index. Classify each chip by content pattern:
   - salary: matches /\$[\d,]+/
   - workplace: matches /Remote|Hybrid|On-site/i
   - employment: matches /Full-time|Part-time|Contract/i
   - experience: matches /Entry|Associate|Mid|Senior|Executive/i
4. Location might not be a chip at all on some layouts — need separate selectors for location (probably aria-label based, similar to company extraction)
5. Consider falling back to parsing salary from description body via regex when no salary chip found

Scope: defer until P4 (extraction -> API write) so we don't pollute the Forge DB with wrong data. P1's goal was proving the wiring; field accuracy for full layout coverage is a P4+ concern.

Blocks: P4 (extraction -> API write) — should be fixed before we start writing to Forge
close_reason: Merged in 7738f18. Chip classification rewritten from positional index to content-pattern regex. Capital One fixture + test added. 10/10 tests pass. Worktree removed.

title: Add MCP tools for creating and updating bullets directly

description:
Review all Education sources for:

4. **Credential linking**: Ensure certifications that came from education programs are linked (e.g. SANS certs → SANS degree).
close_reason: Fixed SANS degree_type trailing comma. Audit findings: 3 HuggingFace courses need dates (deferred per user), edu_description needed for all entries, MCC-KC campus data exists but PDF rendering suppressed by design.

title: Skills taxonomy audit — distribution, categorization, and gaps
description:
Review all Skills at /data/skills for:
1. **Category distribution**: Are skills evenly distributed or are some categories bloated (e.g. 'other') while others are thin?
2. **Multi-category skills**: Identify skills that could belong in multiple categories (e.g. 'Terraform' is in 'language' but could also be 'infrastructure'; 'MLFlow' is in 'infrastructure' but could be 'ai_ml').
3. **Category fitness**: For each category, review every member. Flag misplacements (e.g. 'RAG Pipelines' was in infrastructure, 'Linkerd' vs 'Service Mesh').
4. **Missing categories**: Are there natural groupings not represented? (e.g. 'cloud_platforms' vs lumping AWS/Azure/GCP into 'platform' with AppDynamics/Datadog).
5. **Missing skills**: Based on resume bullets and JD requirements, are there skills that should exist but don't?
Uses the new skill_categories table from migration 044.
close_reason: Reclassified all 14 'other' skills to proper categories (0 remaining in other). Key findings documented: 112 skills with no bullet links, 7 resume skills without backing, Cloud/AWS naming tangle — all deferred to dr4.

title: Clean up perspectives linked to rejected bullets
description:
After the bullet dedup (47 rejections), some rejected bullets still have approved or pending perspectives attached. For each rejected bullet:
1. Check if it has perspectives (approved, pending, or draft).
2. If the perspective content is unique and valuable, migrate it to the surviving bullet in the dedup cluster (the one we kept).
3. If the perspective is a duplicate of one already on the surviving bullet, reject it.
4. If the perspective has content not captured anywhere else, extract it as a note or new bullet before rejecting.
Goal: No approved perspectives should point to rejected bullets.
close_reason: Migrated all 43 orphaned perspectives to surviving bullets. 0 rejections needed — every perspective had a matching survivor. 16 resume entries across 4 resumes (Anthropic Agent Infra, Sandboxing, Public Sector, Snorkel AI) now point to valid bullets.

title: Domains and archetypes taxonomy audit
description:
Review all Domains at /data/domains and Archetypes for:
1. **Domain descriptions**: Current domains have brief descriptions. Expand with clear scope definitions and examples of what belongs in each.
2. **Domain completeness**: Are there missing domains? (e.g. 'mlops' appears as a bullet domain but not in the domains table; 'data-engineering' is used in bullets but may not be a formal domain).
3. **Archetype-domain mapping**: Review which domains each archetype expects. Are mappings complete? (e.g. 'ml-engineer' archetype doesn't exist — resume uses it but it's not in the archetypes table).
4. **Archetype completeness**: Are there missing archetypes for target roles? (e.g. 'ml-engineer', 'data-engineer', 'devops-engineer').
5. **Category fitness**: Same analysis as skills — do all domain members fit? Are there better groupings?
close_reason: Created ml-engineer/devops-engineer/research-engineer archetypes, dropped hft. Added descriptions to 5 domains. Remaining design questions (domain merges, empty-archetype resumes) deferred to dr4.

title: Audit roles for bullet overlap and coverage gaps
description:
Review all Roles at /experience/roles for:
1. **Cross-role overlap**: Compare bullets across roles at the same employer (especially Raytheon's 3 roles) and across employers. Identify where the same work is claimed under multiple roles and recommend which role should own it.
2. **Bullet coverage**: For each role, check if it has derived bullets. Flag roles with zero bullets or thin coverage (e.g. only 1-2 bullets for a multi-year role).
3. **Lineage integrity**: Verify role→bullet→perspective chains are intact. Flag orphaned bullets (no source) or roles whose bullets were rejected in dedup but not replaced.
Known issue: Raytheon Cloud Forensics Engineer and Principal Cloud Forensics Engineer share tool-evaluation and data-ingestion bullets.
close_reason: Audit complete. Cisco (3 bullets) and Raytheon Cloud Forensics Eng (5 bullets) flagged as thin — bullet derivation sessions planned. Principal near-dupes deferred. No zero-coverage roles found.

title: Audit projects for source data completeness and bullet coverage
description:
Review all Project sources at /experience/projects for:
1. **Source data completeness**: Many projects have thin descriptions (1-2 sentences). Expand descriptions with technical details, architecture decisions, and outcomes to support richer bullet derivation.
2. **Bullet coverage**: Check which projects have derived bullets vs which are still just a description. Flag projects with zero bullets.
3. **Cross-project overlap**: Some projects overlap with role work (e.g. MLOps pipeline exists as both a role bullet and a project source). Clarify ownership.
4. **Snorkel-relevant projects**: Sanskrit, Bespin, Forge, and LLM Agent Experiment Platform are on the current resume. Ensure their source data is rich enough to derive multiple perspective variants.
This will require significant investment in expanding project source descriptions.

title: Presentation bullet edits fail with 'Entry not found' — synthetic IDs not in DB
description: The resume compiler generates synthetic UUIDs (e.g. 752a6c59-752a-452a-a52a-752a6c590000) for presentation bullet sub-items. These IDs are returned in the compiled IR but don't exist in the resume_entries table. When the web UI tries to PATCH /api/resumes/:id/entries/:syntheticId, it gets 404 NOT_FOUND. The real entry is the parent presentation entry (e.g. c45a730f). Fix options: (A) Create real child entries for presentation bullets, (B) Route edits to the parent entry, (C) Make the UI aware of synthetic vs real IDs.

title: Extend perspectives to cover project sources
description: Currently project entries on resumes use direct content overrides on resume_entries, bypassing the bullet→perspective→resume_entry pipeline that experience entries use. Projects should support the same derivation flow: derive perspectives from project source descriptions, approve them, and add them to resumes via perspective_id. This would enable versioned, archetype-aware project descriptions and consistent provenance tracking.

title: Skills category drag-n-drop reorder in resume editor
description: Feature 5 from 2026-04-05 session: make skill categories (framework, language, methodology, tool, etc.) reorderable via drag-and-drop in the DragNDropView. svelte-dnd-action already used for experience bullets. Adding/removing skills stays on '+ Add Entry'. Deferred to a separate session.

title: Per-resume certification selection (replace auto-include-all)
description:
Phase 88's buildCertificationItems auto-includes ALL certifications in any resume with a Certifications section. Need per-resume control:

Option A (quick): show_on_resume boolean on certifications — per-cert global toggle
Option B (proper): resume_certifications junction table — per-resume cert picker + remove

Requires brainstorm → spec → plan. Related to the Phase 88 Qualifications architectural shift.

title: Design: reusable error indicator + issue resolution modal
description:
Two reusable design-system components deferred from the 2026-04-05 session:

1. Issue/Error Modal — surface page-level data problems (e.g. 'N unlinked role sources') with fast-path resolution actions. Reusable across sources, resumes, orgs pages.

2. Alert Indicator — inline warning bubble/badge on Entry components (padded, unpadded), inputs, selects, buttons. Reusable like a CSS class + Svelte component. Highlights when an entry has a problem (missing org, expired cert, etc).

Both should share visual language (severity levels, problem → resolution interaction pattern). Brainstorm together as a 'data quality indicator' design system spec.

title: Skills public/private flag — lock public skills from user edits
description:
Add a public/private flag to skills. Public skills are from a shared taxonomy (e.g., well-known technologies, certifications) and should not be renamed or recategorized by the user. Private skills are user-created and fully editable.

## Motivation
Prevents accidental edits to canonical skill names (renaming "Kubernetes" to "K8s" would break pattern matching and gap analysis). Also enables future skill sharing/import from a community taxonomy.

## Proposed
- Add `is_public BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` to skills table
- Public skills: name, category, and relations are read-only via UI/API
- Users can still link/unlink public skills to bullets, resumes, etc.
- Skill creation defaults to private; import/seed scripts set public

title: Bullets mindmap/sankey visualization
description:
Add an alternative view mode to /bullets that shows bullets as a mindmap or sankey diagram. Visualize the flow from sources → bullets → perspectives, showing how content derives and branches across the pipeline.

## Motivation
The current list view makes it hard to see the derivation tree. A visual graph would help identify:
- Sources with many bullets vs few
- Bullets that have been derived into multiple perspectives
- Orphaned bullets with no perspectives
- Coverage density across domains/archetypes

title: Skills graph view — Obsidian-style link map with clusters
description:
Add a graph visualization to /skills showing skills as nodes with edges representing relationships (domains, co-occurrence in sources, category grouping). Similar to Obsidian's graph view with cluster detection.

## Desired behavior
- Nodes = skills, sized by usage frequency (bullet links, resume appearances)
- Edges = relationships (same domain, co-occurrence in bullet_sources, category)
- Clusters = skill categories or domains, visually grouped
- Interactive: click a node to see the skill detail, drag to rearrange
- Filter by category, domain, or usage threshold

## Depends on
- Skill relations (job-hunting-TBD) for richer edge types beyond co-occurrence

title: Phase 4: DuckPGQ Adapter
description:
Implement DuckPGQ adapter for read-only analytics over existing SQLite data. SQL/PGQ (ISO SQL:2023) property graph queries via DuckDB's ATTACH mechanism — zero migration for read path. Target: analytics dashboards, coverage scoring, alignment reports.

## Context
Phase 0-2 complete. The StorageAdapter interface supports read-only adapters (AnalyticsStore pattern). DuckPGQ uses CREATE PROPERTY GRAPH over ATTACH'd SQLite tables for graph pattern matching. Key unknown: does CREATE PROPERTY GRAPH work over ATTACH'd SQLite? Needs validation.
design: See spec: .claude/plans/forge-resume-builder/refs/specs/2026-04-08-storage-abstraction-spike-design.md (Phase 4 section)
acceptance_criteria:
- DuckPgqAdapter implements StorageAdapter (read-only subset)
- EntityStore is read-only via ATTACH SQLite + DuckDB queries
- GraphStore uses CREATE PROPERTY GRAPH + SQL/PGQ pattern matching
- VectorStore not implemented (or delegates to DuckDB vss extension)
- AnalyticsStore uses DuckDB columnar engine for aggregation/scoring
- Validates ATTACH'd SQLite interop (critical unknown)
- Property graph DDL covers Forge schema (vertices + edges)
- Deployment exploration doc: embedded vs service vs CDN (CF R2)

title: Phase 3: GraphQLite Adapter
description:
Implement GraphQLite adapter for OSS/self-hosted Forge deployments. Cypher-based graph overlay on existing SQLite file — zero infra, single-file deployment. Pairs with sqlite-vec for vector search.

## Context
Phase 0 (adapter interface), Phase 1 (SQLite adapter), and Phase 2 (HelixDB adapter) are complete and merged. The StorageAdapter interface and EntityLifecycleManager are stable. This adapter delegates EntityStore to SQLite and adds GraphStore via Cypher queries through the GraphQLite SQLite extension.
design: See spec: .claude/plans/forge-resume-builder/refs/specs/2026-04-08-storage-abstraction-spike-design.md (Phase 3 section)
acceptance_criteria:
- GraphQLiteAdapter implements StorageAdapter interface
- EntityStore delegates to SQLite (relational tables are source of truth)
- GraphStore uses Cypher via SELECT cypher(...) over GraphQLite extension
- VectorStore delegates to sqlite-vec extension (if available) or brute-force fallback
- Extension loading works on macOS and Linux
- Graph sync mechanism mirrors FK/junction data as GraphQLite edges
- Integration tests pass against real GraphQLite extension
- Round-trip migration test: sqlite → graphqlite → sqlite

title: Resume compiler profile fetch refactor
description:
## Original Goal

The resume compiler at `packages/core/src/services/resume-compiler.ts` ~line 117 does 3 raw SQL queries to fetch the user profile, then manually JOINs address + URLs:

```ts
const profileRow = db.query('SELECT * FROM user_profile LIMIT 1').get()
const address = db.query('SELECT * FROM addresses WHERE id = ?').get(profileRow.address_id)
const urls = db.query('SELECT * FROM profile_urls WHERE profile_id = ? ORDER BY position').all(profileRow.id)
profile = { ...profileRow, address, urls }
```

This duplicates the exact logic in `ProfileService.fetchSingleton()`. Goal: eliminate the duplication.

## Current Context

**Compiler is sync, ProfileService is async.** `compileResumeIR(db, resumeId, elm?)` is a synchronous function. `ProfileService.getProfile()` returns `Promise<Result<UserProfile>>` (ELM is async). Can't call it without making the compiler async.

**Making the compiler async is high-churn.** ~70 test call sites + 5 production callers (ResumeService, ExportService) would need `await`. Callers are already async, but tests all call `compileResumeIR(db, resumeId)` synchronously.

**The compiler already has an optional `elm` param** but doesn't use it for profile fetching.

## Options Identified

**A) Full async conversion** — Make compiler async, use ProfileService.getProfile(). High churn (~70 test sites), but fully eliminates raw SQL for profile.

**B) Sync helper extraction** — Extract the 3 raw SQL queries into a `fetchProfileSync(db)` helper. Keeps compiler sync, reduces inline mess, but still duplicates ProfileService logic.

**C) Accept the duplication** — The raw SQL works, is tested, and is unlikely to change. The "refactor" may not be worth the churn. Revisit when the compiler gets a larger async rewrite (Phase 2 HelixDB work will force this anyway).

## Decision Needed

Have a conversation to decide which option (or a hybrid) is the right trade-off given the Phase 2 HelixDB work on the horizon.

title: Extension: in-memory session cache for org resolution + URL dedup
description:
Add lightweight in-memory caching in the background service worker to avoid redundant API calls during a session.

**What to cache:**
- Org search results: Map<companyName, orgId> — same company name → same org within a session
- Org creation results: add to org cache after create
- URL dedup positive hits: Set<canonicalUrl> — known captured URLs return 'Already captured' instantly

**Implementation:**
- Simple Map/Set in service worker memory (not chrome.storage.local)
- Cleared automatically on service worker restart (correct TTL = session)
- ~2KB for 50 entries — negligible memory impact
- Cross-browser safe (Map/Set work in all browsers)

**What NOT to cache:**
- Health check (must reflect real-time state)
- Negative lookupByUrl results (JD might be created via webui between checks)
- Full org list (large, changes frequently)
- JD creation results (write operation, no re-read needed)

**Performance impact:**
- Second click on same page → instant 'Already captured' (no HTTP)
- Multiple JDs from same company → org resolution from cache (skip GET /api/organizations)
- Reduces 2-4 HTTP round trips to 0-1 for repeat operations

title: LinkedIn plugin: extract external Apply link URL
description:
LinkedIn job posts often have an external 'Apply' button that goes through linkedin.com/safety/go/?url=<encoded_url>. The plugin should:

1. Find the Apply button/link on the job detail page
2. Extract the href (linkedin.com/safety/go/?url=...)
3. Decode the url query param to get the actual destination
4. Store as a new apply_url field on ExtractedJob

Example:
- Raw link: https://www.linkedin.com/safety/go/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fjob-boards.greenhouse.io%2Fanthropic%2Fjobs%2F4982193008
- Extracted: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/anthropic/jobs/4982193008

Useful for: direct application links in JD records, future Greenhouse plugin, P5 dedup.

title: L3: Small model fallback for ambiguous sections (optional)
description:
For sections that L1+L2 classify with 'low' confidence, run through a small model for classification. Two options to evaluate:

Option A: Fine-tuned MiniLM/DistilBERT (~50MB ONNX) - fast, browser-compatible via ONNX Runtime Web (WASM). Needs training data (~500-1000 labeled JD sections).

Option B: Qwen2.5-0.5B via WebLLM (~300MB) - general purpose, no training data needed, but slower and larger. Uses WebGPU.

This is an enhancement layer - L1+L2 should handle 80-90% of JDs without it. Only build if the heuristic approach proves insufficient.

Decision gate: After L1+L2 are implemented, measure classification accuracy across 50+ real JDs. If accuracy is >90%, skip L3.
